facts.statement: Oracle is listed in SEC submissions metadata as ORACLE CORP with SIC description "Services-Prepackaged Software," exchanges listed as NYSE, and tickers ORCL and ORCL-PD.
Oracle reported total revenues of $57.399 billion for fiscal 2025, compared with $52.961 billion in fiscal 2024 and $49.954 billion in fiscal 2023.
Oracle reported fiscal 2025 net income of $12.443 billion, basic EPS of $4.46, and diluted EPS of $4.34.
The SEC company facts summary reports Oracle fiscal 2025 operating cash flow of $17.165 billion and cash and equivalents of $10.454 billion, filed June 18, 2025.

Oracle presents summary results for three businesses: cloud and license, hardware, and services; the company states it does not track assets for each business and therefore it is not practical to show assets by operating segment.

Oracle's cloud and license business generated fiscal 2025 revenues of $49.230 billion, with reported segment margin of $30.930 billion before allocations and excluded expenses described in the filing.

Oracle states that costs associated with its cloud and license business are largely personnel- and infrastructure-related, including costs to provide cloud services and license support, sales-force salaries and commissions, and marketing program costs.
Oracle's fiscal 2025 cloud services and license support revenues totaled $44.029 billion, consisting of $24.506 billion of cloud services revenues and $19.523 billion of license support revenues.

Within cloud services and license support revenues in fiscal 2025, Oracle reported $19.383 billion from applications cloud services and license support and $24.646 billion from infrastructure cloud services and license support.

Oracle's hardware business provides infrastructure technologies including Oracle Engineered Systems, servers, storage, industry-specific hardware, operating systems, virtualization, management, and other hardware-related software.
Oracle's hardware business generated fiscal 2025 revenues of $2.936 billion and reported segment margin of $1.918 billion before allocations and excluded expenses described in the filing.

Oracle says its hardware support can include software updates for software components essential to hardware functionality, product repairs, maintenance services, and technical support services, typically delivered and recognized ratably over the contractual term.
Oracle's services business provides services to customers and partners to help maximize the performance of their investments in Oracle applications and infrastructure technologies.
Oracle's services business generated fiscal 2025 revenues of $5.233 billion and reported segment margin of $993 million before allocations and excluded expenses described in the filing.

Oracle's reported operating-segment margins reflect only direct controllable costs and exclude allocations of R&D, general and administrative expenses, amortization of intangible assets, acquisition-related and other expenses, restructuring expenses, stock-based compensation, interest expense, and certain non-operating income or expenses.

In the reconciliation from operating-segment margin to income before taxes, Oracle reported fiscal 2025 research and development expense of $9.860 billion and general and administrative expense of $1.602 billion.
Oracle reported fiscal 2025 interest expense of $3.578 billion in its reconciliation from operating-segment margin to income before income taxes.
Oracle's fiscal 2025 revenues by region were $36.339 billion in the Americas, $14.025 billion in EMEA, and $7.035 billion in Asia Pacific.

For fiscal 2025, Oracle reported U.S. revenues of $32.075 billion and U.S. long-lived assets of $45.439 billion; the filing defines long-lived assets as excluding goodwill, intangible assets, non-marketable investments, and deferred taxes.
Oracle's 10-K exhibit index references a $6.000 billion 5-year revolving credit agreement dated March 8, 2022 and a $5.630 billion term loan credit agreement dated June 10, 2024.

Oracle's fiscal 2025 Form 10-K was signed by Safra A. Catz as Chief Executive Officer and Director and Principal Executive and Financial Officer; Maria Smith as Executive Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer; and Lawrence J. Ellison as Chairman of the Board of Directors and Chief Technology Officer.

The proxy states that on September 22, 2025, Safra A. Catz retired from her CEO position and was appointed Executive Vice Chair of the Board; Clayton M. Magouyrk and Michael D. Sicilia were appointed CEOs of Oracle; and Douglas Kehring was appointed Executive Vice President and Principal Financial Officer.

For the 2025 annual meeting, Oracle asked stockholders to elect 13 director nominees, including Lawrence J. Ellison, Safra A. Catz, Clayton M. Magouyrk, Michael D. Sicilia, Jeffrey O. Henley, and eight other nominees listed in the proxy.

Oracle's Corporate Governance Guidelines include a majority voting and mandatory resignation policy requiring a director nominee in an uncontested election to tender resignation if votes withheld are equal to or greater than votes for that nominee and no successor has been elected, with the Board to act within 90 days after vote certification.

Oracle disclosed a Netherlands privacy class action filed August 14, 2020 alleging that the Oracle Defendants' Data Management Platform product violates certain EU and Dutch privacy-related laws; Oracle states it believes it has meritorious defenses, cannot estimate a range of loss, and does not believe the matter will have a material impact on its financial position or results of operations.
